title: rosa glauca subsp. pouzinii
intro: Rosa glauca subsp. pouzinii, also known as the Alpine Sweetbriar, is a subspecies of the Rosa glauca plant, a type of wild rose native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rosa glauca subsp. pouzinii are said to include reduced inflammation, improved cardiovascular health, and antioxidant protection against cell damage. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, with traditional herbal preparations often combining it with other plants to enhance its medicinal properties.
